text.title: JPA/Hibernate Failure Modes
text.summary: Four JPA/Hibernate antipatterns observed in production. Symptom + root cause + fix per item.

antipattern.symptom: `spring.jpa.open-in-view=true` (Spring Boot default). Lazy collections accessed during JSON serialisation fire queries inside the request thread; p99 latency inflates; LazyInit errors hide.
antipattern.root-cause: OSIV kept session open across the full HTTP lifecycle.
antipattern.fix: Set `spring.jpa.open-in-view=false`. Eager-load required associations inside the service via `JOIN FETCH` / `@EntityGraph`. Map to DTOs before returning to the controller.

antipattern.symptom: `@OneToMany(cascade = CascadeType.ALL)` on `Customer.orders`. Deleting a customer wipes their order history including paid invoices. Compliance violation + customer-trust event.
antipattern.root-cause: `CascadeType.ALL` applied without justification; ownership not analyzed.
antipattern.fix: Replace with narrower cascade set (e.g., `PERSIST, MERGE` only) + write a Javadoc justification. Orphan removal only where the child genuinely cannot exist without parent.

antipattern.symptom: Service returns entity; controller serialises it; `LazyInitializationException` fires on a nested collection.
antipattern.root-cause: Lazy collection accessed after transaction close.
antipattern.fix: Either: (a) load the association via `JOIN FETCH` in the repository method, OR (b) map to a DTO inside the transactional service so the controller never touches the entity graph.

antipattern.symptom: List endpoint loads 100 orders; iterating `order.items` triggers 100 additional `SELECT * FROM items WHERE order_id = ?`. Endpoint takes 4s.
antipattern.root-cause: Repository method `findAll()` returned entities; caller iterated collections without `JOIN FETCH`.
antipattern.fix: Add a repository method with `@Query("SELECT o FROM Order o JOIN FETCH o.items")` or project directly to a DTO with the required fields.
